join_order.build: pre(adsh range directory over sorted adsh_tag_version index)
join_order.probe: num(filtered rows in surviving ddate blocks)
join_order.notes: Build a dense adsh->rowid-range directory from pre/indexes/adsh_tag_version.rowids.bin and pre/adsh.bin. For each qualifying num row, use adsh to jump directly to the matching pre slice, then binary-search only that slice on (tag,version) instead of global binary-searching all 9.6M pre rows.

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current anti-join does about 9.0M probes after filtering, and each probe performs a global binary search over a pre membership structure whose rowids plus key columns span about 154 MB, well beyond the 88 MB LLC, so probe time is dominated by cache-miss-heavy comparisons. Strategy category A/B: keep the sequential num scan and ddate block pruning, but restructure the join to exploit the existing sort prefix on pre.adsh by first building a dense direct-array directory from adsh id to [start,end) in the sorted pre rowid index, then probing only that adsh-local slice for (tag,version); this shrinks each lookup from ~23 global comparisons across four large arrays to ~7 comparisons inside an average 111-row slice and removes pre.adsh from the hot probe path.
